title: Nominate outstanding public servants for the 2025 National Batho Pele and Innovation Awards!
description: BUSINESS NEWS - The Minister for the Public...
bodyText:
Sponsored Content

BUSINESS NEWS - The Minister for the Public Service and Administration, Inkosi Mzamo Buthelezi, and Deputy Minister Pinky Kekana invite all citizens to recognize and celebrate the efforts of public servants dedicated to delivering exceptional services.

The 2025 National Batho Pele and Innovation Awards are now open for nominations, and this is your chance to highlight those who go the extra mile in serving our communities.


> How to nominate


Nominating an outstanding public servant is simple:

- Visit the NBPIA nomination portal https://linktr.ee/NBPIAWARDS
- Complete the online nomination form with the details of the public servant you wish to recognise, and
- Submit your entry before the deadline of 19 September 2025.

Your nomination is an act of appreciation and respect for the dedication of those who often work quietly in the background, ensuring that services are delivered fairly, effectively, and with care.


> Why nominate?


These awards are not just accolades; they are an opportunity to promote a culture of excellence, innovation, and accountability within the public service sector.

By nominating public servants who exemplify these values, you contribute to a movement that encourages continuous improvement in service delivery, ultimately benefiting all South Africans.

Public servants often face numerous challenges, yet many rise above these to ensure that citizens receive the best possible service. This is your chance to honor their hard work and dedication.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
